title: Writing Your First Function
description: Learn the syntax of defining and calling functions in R.

slides.title: Why Functions? 🛠️
slides.content:
# Functions

Functions let you package a block of code that performs a specific task, so you can reuse it without rewriting. They are the building blocks of R programs.

A function takes **inputs** (arguments), does something, and often returns an **output**.

slides.title: Function Syntax
Your First Function
slides.content: Define a function that adds two numbers:

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `return()` in a function?

options.text: To exit the function and send back a value
To stop the function if there's an error

quizData.explanation: return() specifies the value to be returned and exits the function.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: In R, the last evaluated expression is automatically returned, so you can omit `return()` for simplicity, but using it makes your intent clear.
